hero.headline: Builds things that ship.
hero.subline: Full-stack products, native apps, and the infrastructure under them, built solo and taken all the way to real users.
hero.kicker: Niranjan Rakesh, engineer in the UAE

about.lead: I build complete things: products with real users, uptime to keep, and rough edges worn down by actual use. Prototypes that die in a folder don't count.
body: I trained in Computer Science and I'm comfortable below the framework: I've shipped a production backend with zero npm dependencies, hand-rolled drag-and-drop from raw pointer events, and tuned video sync for connections that misbehave.

I started out in design, and it still decides what I build. Most of my engineering time goes into how things feel to use, like an input that accepts whatever you paste into it, or an app that opens straight to the point.

themes.title: Ships complete things
themes.detail: Takes an idea to a running product with real infrastructure under it, end to end, solo.

themes.title: Depth over glue
themes.detail: Zero-dependency servers, custom sync logic, raw pointer events. Goes below the framework when the framework is the problem.

themes.title: Product judgment
themes.detail: Decides by how the thing feels to use, not by what's easiest to wire together.

smallerBuilds.name: Flixify
smallerBuilds.line: Native macOS drag-and-drop transcoder that preps uploads for PookieFlix, a self-hosted streaming site: hardware VideoToolbox H.264, bundled ffmpeg, shipped as a signed and notarized DMG.

smallerBuilds.name: PerfectTasks
smallerBuilds.line: Full-stack task manager (Next.js, Drizzle, Clerk) designed, planned, and built test-first in a single day.

smallerBuilds.name: orbit-core
smallerBuilds.line: NestJS + Prisma backend for time management: OAuth, Google Calendar sync, multi-device conflict resolution, BullMQ jobs.

footer.line: Designed and built from scratch by Niranjan Rakesh.
